data.name: Portable_ServiceStatus
data.value: Portable mode: running without the RigPilot service. Hardware data is read-only; profiles, automation, and every hardware write are unavailable.

data.name: Portable_SafetySummary
data.value: Portable mode is read-only by design: monitoring runs in this process and no hardware write path exists.

data.name: Portable_UpdateStatus
data.value: Driver updates are unavailable in portable mode.

data.name: Onboarding_Body1
data.value: RigPilot controls fans, pumps, GPU limits, lighting, and profiles from one place. Every hardware write is transactional: the previous state is saved first, the applied value is read back from the hardware to verify it, and a failed write is rolled back. Built-in profiles stay within stock limits.

data.name: Onboarding_Body2
data.value: The Hardware control switch in the dashboard header is the master arm for every writable control. While it is on, RigPilot may apply the values you set, always inside vendor bounds. Turning it off disarms every control and restores vendor defaults; stopping the service does the same. Voltage is never increased automatically.

data.name: Onboarding_Body3
data.value: Every control carries an evidence label. Verified means apply, read-back, and reset were proven on real hardware. Experimental means it works but has limited evidence — use it knowingly. Read Only and Blocked controls cannot write at all. The Devices page shows the evidence and the next safe step for each control.

data.name: Updates_CardBody
data.value: Checks the public GitHub releases page for a newer version. Nothing is downloaded or installed automatically, and the Windows service never touches the network.

data.name: Updates_NotCheckedYet
data.value: No check has run yet this session.

data.name: Updates_Checking
data.value: Checking github.com for the latest release…
